market_overview.headline: Markets in broad-based bear structure as SPY, QQQ, and BTC all show Full Bear signals amid Iran war escalation.
market_overview.detail: Only 24% of stocks in Full Bull (72 of 300), while 38% are Full Bear (113 stocks). All major indices below their weekly EMAs with potential bullish crossover alerts suggesting oversold conditions.

reversal_candidates.headline: Early Reversal Signals - Bear Rallies with Crossover Alerts

items.note: Mega-cap AI leader showing early EMA convergence after -6.8% decline from 21W average

momentum_themes.headline: Storage and Optical Networks Lead Momentum
momentum_themes.detail: Storage stocks (SNDK, WDC) and optical networking (LITE, CIEN) showing explosive momentum with 20%+ gains from 21W EMAs. Defense and energy sectors maintaining strong momentum amid geopolitical tensions.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominates, Tech Services Collapse

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score with all 15 stocks in Full Bull amid Iran war premium
Defense and oilfield services benefiting from geopolitical tensions
Defensive sector holding up well in risk-off environment

weakest.note: Massive tech selloff with 91% of stocks in bearish structure
Banking sector crushed with zero Full Bull stocks and 83% bearish
Healthcare providers under pressure with no bullish names

sector_analysis.detail: Clear flight to safety with energy/defense/utilities outperforming while tech and financials collapse. Classic war-time sector rotation pattern.

items.text: 38% of stocks in Full Bear structure - broadest deterioration across all indices
Tech megacaps (MSFT -17.4%, META -17.9%, CRM -17.2%) showing severe damage
Finance sector completely broken with zero Full Bull stocks and 83% bearish
36 Bull Breakdown signals indicate established uptrends starting to crack

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran war escalation dominated headlines with missile strikes on Israeli and Gulf infrastructure driving energy prices higher and tech selling. Pentagon preparing for extended ground operations while diplomatic talks in Pakistan seek de-escalation.

items.text: Iranian missile strikes on Israeli chemical plants and Gulf aluminum facilities sparked war premium in energy stocks
Pentagon reportedly preparing for weeks of ground operations in Iran, boosting defense contractors
Strait of Hormuz tensions disrupted LPG tanker routes, further supporting energy sector momentum
Tech megacaps sold off as investors fled risk assets for defensive energy and utility plays
Pakistan hosting regional diplomatic talks offering some hope for de-escalation amid market chaos
